meaning: n.驮马,做粗工者,重负荷机器
examples: For years the Apple II had been the workhorse in schools across America.
The Resource Description Framework (RDF) is the workhorse of the Semantic Web.
But one of Linux's most important strengths is serving as the workhorse of the storage domain.
The workhorse military vehicles used in Iraq and Afghanistan are not a part of the deal.
Super fortunate to have been aligned with great complementary defensive players and a workhorse running back.
